mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth, compressing corporate profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 40% of observed instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active Federal Reserve tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of cooling consumer credit on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp alongside declining RSAFS, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to exhibit a positive correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for three consecutive months, robust hiring validates strong underlying demand, which reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200.0 for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 10-year backtest, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coinciding with stable short-term rates indicates healthy supply-side expansion without monetary friction, which supports corporate margin growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and stable FEDFUNDS fails to predict positive SPX returns in more than 45% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by peak housing starts, further declines in consumer credit signal demand exhaustion, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where HOUST drops below 600 alongside declining RSAFS, or the joint signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 5-year walk-forward test.
